---
title: "Getting started with bivnegbincp"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting started with bivnegbincp}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  \usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

# Introduction

`bivnegbincp` implements Bayesian multiple change-point detection for bivariate negative binomial count data.

## Load the package
```{r}
library(bivnegbincp)
```

## Simulated example
```{r}
set.seed(123)

x1 <- c(
  rnbinom(50, size = 10, mu = 5),
  rnbinom(25, size = 10, mu = 12),
  rnbinom(25, size = 10, mu = 20)
)

x2 <- c(
  rnbinom(50, size = 8, mu = 4),
  rnbinom(25, size = 8, mu = 10),
  rnbinom(25, size = 8, mu = 18)
)
```

## Run change-point detection
```{r}
res <- bivnegbin_singlecp(
  x1 = x1,
  x2 = x2
)
```


## Inspect output
```{r}
names(res)
str(res, max.level = 1)
```
